name: ODE-Based Habit Strength with Continuous Arbitration Dynamics
description: Continuous-time ODE-inspired dual-system agent where habit strength, goal-directed value, and a depletable cognitive control capacity co-evolve over discrete Euler time steps. Unlike Formulations 1 and 2 which blend two value streams into a single Q-net, this formulation implements stochastic system selection: at each step, the agent probabilistically selects EITHER the goal-directed system OR the habitual system based on current cognitive control capacity C_t, then applies softmax within the selected system. Habit strengths H(s,a) grow via an ODE-like accumulation driven by positive reward and decay when unused. Cognitive control capacity C_t depletes each time the goal-directed system is engaged (modeling PFC executive effort cost) and recovers when habitual control is used. This produces emergent overtraining-to-habit dynamics: as habits strengthen and C_t depletes from repeated deliberation, the agent naturally falls back onto habitual control. Based on Rangel, Camerer & Montague (2008) and Rangel (2013), postulates P1–P5, with inspiration from ego-depletion / executive resource frameworks.

variables.name: state
variables.description: Agent's current grid cell encoded as (x, y) tuple

variables.name: habit_strength
variables.description: Continuously evolving stimulus-response association strength for each (state, action) pair. Grows when the action is executed and produces positive reward; decays slowly when not used. High H means strong habitual tendency. Not sensitive to current desirability — reflects historical reinforcement only (postulate P3).

variables.name: goal_directed_value
variables.description: Prospectively computed action value from the learned transition model and current hunger-modulated desirability. Recomputed every step. Immediately sensitive to changes in hunger / outcome value (postulate P2).

variables.name: cognitive_control_capacity
variables.description: Depletable resource representing prefrontal executive function. Depleted by xi each time goal-directed deliberation is used; recovers toward C_max at rate rho when habitual control is used. When C < theta, goal-directed system cannot be engaged. Models the computational/metabolic cost of model-based planning (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008).

variables.name: hunger_drive
variables.description: Internal physiological state representing current need. Modulates goal-directed desirability r_D. Rises each step, drops upon eating.

variables.name: decision_time_desirability
variables.description: Current subjective value of each possible outcome, modulated by hunger. Food: r_D = h * r_food. Non-food: r_D = c_step.

variables.name: learned_transition_model
variables.description: Agent's estimate of action-outcome contingency p_hat(o|a,s). Updated incrementally from observations. Core of the goal-directed system.

variables.name: composite_action_urgency
variables.description: Action urgency signal used for final softmax selection. Set to either V_GD(s,a) or H(s,a) depending on which system was stochastically selected for this step.

variables.name: goal_directed_probability
variables.description: Probability of engaging the goal-directed system on this step. Equals C_t if C_t >= theta, else 0. Captures the gating role of cognitive control capacity.

parameters.name: habit_accumulation_rate
parameters.source: Slow growth of S-R strength reflecting overtraining timescale (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008; postulate P4)

parameters.name: habit_decay_rate
parameters.source: Slow decay of unused habits; habits are persistent but not permanent

parameters.name: transition_model_learning_rate
parameters.source: Rapid model update for goal-directed system (Rangel, 2013; postulate P2)

parameters.name: max_cognitive_control_capacity
parameters.source: Normalized upper bound for cognitive control resource

parameters.name: control_recovery_rate
parameters.source: Per-step recovery toward C_max when habitual action is taken; reflects PFC resource replenishment

parameters.name: control_depletion_per_gd_use
parameters.source: Cost of using goal-directed deliberation; reflects computational expense of model-based planning (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008)

parameters.name: override_threshold
parameters.source: Minimum C_t required to engage goal-directed system; below this, only habitual control is available

parameters.name: hunger_rise_rate
parameters.source: Per-step hunger increase when not eating

parameters.name: base_food_reward
parameters.source: Normalized reward unit matching env_spec ConsumeEffect reward of 1.0

parameters.name: inverse_temperature
parameters.source: Moderate exploitation for softmax within the selected system (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008)

rules.description: Hunger dynamics: hunger rises each step by eta and drops by phi when food is consumed. Clipped to [0, 1].
rules.source_postulate: Assumption: state-dependent desirability; internal hunger modulates goal-directed valuation

rules.description: Decision-time desirability: subjective value of food outcome is hunger-modulated; non-food outcome incurs step cost.
rules.source_postulate: P2 — goal-directed value uses current desirability r^D(o|s)

rules.description: Goal-directed value (prospective): computed from learned transition model and current desirability. For eat at food: sum of p_hat * r_D. For movement: step cost plus discounted future value. Recomputed every step.

Habit strength ODE (discrete Euler step): H(s,a) accumulates from positive reward when the action is executed in state s, and decays at rate tau_D toward zero for all (s,a) pairs. Only positive rewards drive habit formation ([r]+).
rules.pseudocode:
# For the executed action in current state:
H[(s, a_executed)] = H.get((s, a_executed), 0.0) + tau_H * max(r_received, 0.0) - tau_D * H.get((s, a_executed), 0.0)

# For all other (s, a) pairs, apply passive decay:
for each (s_i, a_i) in H:
  if (s_i, a_i) != (s, a_executed):
    H[(s_i, a_i)] = H[(s_i, a_i)] - tau_D * H[(s_i, a_i)]

# Ensure non-negative:
H[(s_i, a_i)] = max(H[(s_i, a_i)], 0.0)
rules.source_postulate: P3, P4 — habits form slowly from repeated reinforcement; insensitive to current desirability; accumulated S-R association

rules.description: Cognitive control capacity dynamics: C_t recovers toward C_max when habitual control is used (no deliberation cost), and depletes by xi when goal-directed system is engaged. Clipped to [0, C_max].
rules.source_postulate: Rangel, Camerer & Montague (2008) — computational cost of model-based planning; PFC executive resource constraints

rules.description: System selection criterion: probability of engaging goal-directed system is C_t if above threshold theta; otherwise forced habitual. Captures cognitive gating.
rules.source_postulate: P4 — goal-directed control requires cognitive resources; depleted resources force habitual default

rules.description: Composite action urgency via stochastic system selection: with probability p_GD, use goal-directed values V_GD; with probability 1-p_GD, use habit strengths H. This is NOT a blend — it's a binary switch per step.
rules.source_postulate: P1 — two dissociable controllers; not blended but selected (competitive)

rules.description: Softmax action selection over the urgency values from the selected system.
rules.pseudocode:
probs = softmax([beta * U[a] for a in actions])
selected_action = random_choice(actions, weights=probs)
rules.source_postulate: Assumption: common action-selection mechanism (softmax) within selected controller

rules.description: Transition model update: after observing outcome, update p_hat toward observed outcome.
rules.source_postulate: P2 — action-outcome contingency p(o|a,s) is learned and updated from experience

decision_logic.description: Each step: (1) Read state s=(x,y), food resources, hunger h, cognitive control C. (2) Compute hunger-modulated desirability r_D (R2). (3) Check if C >= theta to determine if goal-directed system is available (R6). (4) Stochastically select system: draw uniform random, compare to p_GD (R7). (5) If GD selected: compute V_GD for all actions from transition model and r_D (R3), set U = V_GD. If habitual selected: retrieve H(s,a) for all actions, set U = H. (6) Select action via softmax over U (R8). (7) After execution: update hunger (R1), update habit strength H via ODE step (R4) — executed action gains from positive reward, all habits decay. (8) Update transition model (R9). (9) Update cognitive control C: deplete if GD was used, recover otherwise (R5).
pseudocode: food_positions = [(f['x'], f['y']) for f in perception.resources.get('food', [])]
# R6: Determine p_GD from cognitive control capacity
V_GD_a = p_hat.get((s, a, 'food'), 0.5) * r_D_food + (1 - p_hat.get((s, a, 'food'), 0.5)) * r_D_nofood
best_future_H = max(H.get((s_next, a2), 0.0) for a2 in actions)
V_GD_a = c_step + gamma * best_future_H * r_D_food if best_future_H > 0 else c_step
probs = softmax([beta * U[a] for a in actions])
# Store use_gd flag for update phase
r_received = r_food if ate_food else c_step
# R4: Update habit strengths (ODE Euler step)
# Executed action in current state gets positive reinforcement
H[(s, action)] = max(H.get((s, action), 0.0) + tau_H * r_pos - tau_D * H.get((s, action), 0.0), 0.0)
for (s_i, a_i) in H where (s_i, a_i) != (s, action):
H[(s_i, a_i)] = max(H[(s_i, a_i)] - tau_D * H[(s_i, a_i)], 0.0)
p_hat[(s, action, o_obs)] = p_hat.get((s, action, o_obs), 0.5) + alpha_T * (1.0 - p_hat.get((s, action, o_obs), 0.5))
for o in outcomes where o != o_obs:
p_hat[(s, action, o)] = p_hat.get((s, action, o), 0.5) + alpha_T * (0.0 - p_hat.get((s, action, o), 0.5))
C = clip(C + rho * (C_max - C) - xi, 0.0, C_max)

perception_to_variables.food_positions: perception.resources.food — list of {x, y, type, palatability} dicts
perception_to_variables.food_at_current_position: any(f['x'] == perception.x and f['y'] == perception.y for f in perception.resources.get('food', []))
perception_to_variables.ate_food: action was 'eat' and perception.last_action_result indicates consumption

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action at food location → ConsumeEffect reward = 1.0; all other actions → internal step cost c_step = -0.01

expected_behaviors.description: Initial goal-directed dominance: at step 0, C=1.0 (max), so p_GD = 1.0 and the agent always uses V_GD. The agent makes hunger-sensitive, deliberate food-seeking choices.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: At step 0 → assert C == 1.0 → assert p_GD == 1.0 → assert agent uses V_GD for all decisions

expected_behaviors.description: Cognitive depletion drives habit transition: after many consecutive goal-directed decisions, C depletes below theta, forcing habitual control. The agent starts relying on habit strengths H(s,a).
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Run 20 consecutive GD decisions → C decreases by ~20*xi = 2.0 (but recovery offsets partially) → eventually C < theta → assert p_GD == 0 → assert agent uses H values

expected_behaviors.description: Habit strengths accumulate for rewarded actions: eating at food locations produces positive reward, increasing H(s_food, eat) via the ODE. After many successful eats, H(s_food, eat) becomes large.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Execute 'eat' at food location 50 times → assert H[(food_loc, eat)] > 1.0 → assert H is much larger for eat-at-food than other actions

expected_behaviors.description: Devaluation sensitivity when GD active: if C is high and the agent is sated (h≈0), V_GD for eating is near zero (r_D_food ≈ 0), so the agent avoids eating even when food is present.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Set h=0.05, C=1.0 → assert V_GD[eat] ≈ 0.05 (very low) → assert agent prefers non-eat actions

expected_behaviors.description: Devaluation insensitivity when habitual: if C is low (below theta) and the agent is sated, it falls back on H(s,a). Since H reflects past positive reinforcement (not current hunger), the agent may still eat at food locations where H(s, eat) is high.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Set h=0.05, C=0.05 (< theta) → assert p_GD == 0 → assert U[eat] = H[(s, eat)] which is high from past experience → agent still eats

expected_behaviors.description: Cognitive control recovery during habitual episodes: when the agent uses habitual control (no GD depletion), C recovers toward C_max. After sufficient recovery, the agent can re-engage goal-directed control.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Deplete C to 0.05 → run 30 habitual steps → assert C recovers above theta → assert p_GD > 0 → GD system re-engages

expected_behaviors.description: Habit decay for unused actions: habit strengths for (state, action) pairs that are not executed slowly decay toward zero via the tau_D term, preventing permanent entrenchment.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Build H[(s, eat)] = 1.0 → run 200 steps without visiting s → assert H[(s, eat)] has decayed significantly toward 0

references: Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience, 9, 545–556.
Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717–1724.
